prompt: Write four absurd but coherent sentences in the voice of a confused medieval chronicler who has been hired to document the Q3 results of a modern SaaS company.
response: By the grace of our lord St. Saas, the scribes of the modern company convened in a glass tower to recite the Q3 results. Lo and behold, they spake of something called "MRR" which grew mightily by $400,000! Then they mentioned "ARR" which leaped triumphantly to $5.3 million. The breacher of books, he who doth count and tabulate, rejoiced and exclaimed "CAC is down to a mere 0.84!" Upon revealing "LTV" to be $8.7 million, the scribes and leaders of modern SaaS cried out "Huzzah!" and partook in a feast of pizza and craft brewries to celebrate their bountiful harvest of unicorn dollars.
